etymology.description: 匸 (that which hides) + 矢 (arrow) = a hidden arrow -> metaphor for treating (hidden dangers addressed by medicine)
etymology.interpretation: The character combines the concepts of 'hide' with 'arrow', suggesting the idea of dealing with hidden threats or illnesses, akin to specialized knowledge required for healing.
etymology.simplification: 醫(醫) was simplified by using the right side 耂(耂) (lǎo), an abbreviated structure representing the character.

parts.english: that which hides; that which stores

examples.chinese: 他是一名优秀的医生(他是一名優秀的醫生)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yì míng yōuxiù de yīshēng.

examples.chinese: 我感冒了，需要去医院(我感冒了，需要去醫院)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ gǎnmào le, xūyào qù yīyuàn.
examples.english: I caught a cold and need to go to the hospital.

examples.chinese: 这家医院以先进的医疗技术闻名(這家醫院以先進的醫療技術聞名)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā yīyuàn yǐ xiānjìn de yīliáo jìshù wénmíng.
examples.english: This hospital is famous for its advanced medical technology.
